free_text: The cyclotron beam goes through a uniform
magnetic field, passes through the target under study,
and stops in a Faraday cup. The boundary of the
magnetic field region has the shape of a sector of an
annulus, and the target under study is located at the
center of the annulus defined by the boundary of the
The detector is movable along the outer boundary of the
magnetic field permitting detection of particles over
a range of angles at and near 180 degrees.
No information on source of uncertainties.
